Chamath Explains Why AI is So Unpopular in America: Terrible Communication from Industry Leaders

“ ICE is more popular than AI. That's where AI is.”

Why is this happening? Three reasons according to @chamath:

“Entrepreneurs are A/B testing what it takes to raise money.”

“At least some parts of the AI ecosystem have decided that this crazy, scary doomerism is the best way to raise money.”

“Where they come out and say, ‘All the jobs will be destroyed; this thing is sentient.’ Anthropic, you k

Under Secretary of War Emil Michael:

Emil Michael Reveals the “Holy Sh*t Moment” That Caused the Pentagon-Anthropic Fallout

Under Secretary of War Emil Michael:

“The trigger point was after the Maduro raid.”

“One of their execs called Palantir, who we buy their stuff through, and asked them, ‘Was our software used in that raid?’ Which is, by the way, classified information anyway.”

“So they’re trying to get classified information, and implying that if they were used in that raid, that might violate their terms of service.”

“It

Emil Michael: “If you don’t want your software to be used for Department of War stuff, you shouldn't be selling to the Department of War.”

Under Secretary of War @emilmichael on his frustrations with Anthropic:

“We started these negotiations, and it took three months, and I had to sort of give them scenarios, like this Chinese hypersonic missile example.”

“And they're like, ‘Okay, we'll give you an exception for that.’ Well, how about this drone swarm? ‘We'll give you an exception for that.’”

Anthropic vs the Pentagon: The Inside Story by Emil Michael

The Full Timeline

❓Backstory: Why Anthropic? 

– Anthropic benefited from Biden’s AI executive order, was designated as an early winner

– They smartly used this designation to sell into military and intelligence agencies, with forward-deployed engineers (Palantir style)

– Became deeply integrated into DoW workflows, far ahead of other frontier model competitors

📜Tensions Rise Over Restrictive ToS

– Anthropic's contracts had a long

Ray Dalio's Three Pillars of a Successful Nation

1) Education that fosters productivity and civility

“You have to educate your children well so that they are capable of being productive. And also educate them in civility.”

2) An economic environment that is productive, competitive, and civil

“Then, they have to come out to an environment that is orderly and civil, where people can compete and work with each other to be productive.”

3) No wars

“You have to stay out of wars. No civil war and
